# CleanTreeMac

> Open-source macOS disk space analyzer with sunburst visualization and a deletion basket.

CleanTreeMac scans folders using the system `du` command, builds a `FileNode` tree, and displays sizes in a DaisyDisk-style UI. Users navigate folders, queue items in a basket, and delete to Trash.

## Quick facts

- Language: Swift 5, SwiftUI
- Platform: macOS (native AppKit/SwiftUI)
- Sandbox: disabled (user-level file access)
- Scan engine: `/usr/bin/du -x -k -d N <path>`
- License: open source

## Architecture map

### Entry & UI layer

- CleanTreeMac/CleanTreeMacApp.swift — `@main`, WindowGroup
- CleanTreeMac/ContentView.swift — hosts DiskAnalyzerView
- CleanTreeMac/Views/DiskAnalyzerView.swift — main layout: top bar, chart, list, basket
- CleanTreeMac/Views/SunburstChartView.swift — annular segments from DisplayItem[]
- CleanTreeMac/Views/FileListView.swift — scrollable list + footer (free space rows)
- CleanTreeMac/Views/BreadcrumbView.swift — path breadcrumbs
- CleanTreeMac/Views/BasketView.swift — drag-drop delete queue

### State

- CleanTreeMac/ViewModels/DiskAnalyzerViewModel.swift — @Observable @MainActor
  - Owns DiskIndex, navigation history (back/forward), basket
  - displayItems: chart/list data for current folder
  - startScan → DirectoryScanner.scan with onPartialUpdate for progressive UI
  - navigateInto → DiskIndex lookup; expandFolderIfNeeded → scanFolder

### Models

- CleanTreeMac/Models/FileNode.swift
  - FileNode: id, name, url, size, isDirectory, children, kind (folder | groupedSmall | hiddenSpace | freeSpace | freeAndPurgeable)
  - DisplayItem: wrapper for chart/list coloring
  - BasketItem: queued deletion entry

### Services (core logic — edit here for scan behavior)

- CleanTreeMac/Services/DirectoryScanner.swift
  - scan(url:) — routes "/" to scanMacintoshHD, else scanFolder
  - scanMacintoshHD — stage 1: du d=2 + onPartialUpdate; stage 2: du d=5
  - macintoshHDRoot — splits du tree into priority paths, small objects, hidden space
  - scanFolder — du d=4 for drill-down
  - fallbackFolder / fallbackMacintoshHD — FileManager fallback if du fails
  - footerItems — free space rows at Macintosh HD root

- CleanTreeMac/Services/FolderSizeCalculator.swift
  - fullSnapshot(path:depth:) — single Process: du -x -k -d N path
  - parseTree(from:rootURL:) — stdout → sizeMap + childrenMap
  - buildTree(from:rootURL:) — assembles FileNode tree, children sorted by size desc

- CleanTreeMac/Services/DiskIndex.swift
  - pathMap: [String: FileNode] for O(1) navigation
  - install(root:) — full reindex
  - merge(node:) — replace branch, deindex old, index new, refreshAncestors

- CleanTreeMac/Services/FileDeletionService.swift — FileManager.trashItem
- CleanTreeMac/Services/SystemPaths.swift — systemRoot, macintoshHDPriorityPaths, displayName, specialURL

### Utilities

- CleanTreeMac/Utilities/Formatters.swift — ByteFormat, ChartPalette
- CleanTreeMac/Utilities/AppTheme.swift — light theme colors

## Data flow

1. User opens app → DiskAnalyzerViewModel.launchInitialScan()
2. DirectoryScanner.scanMacintoshHD()
3. FolderSizeCalculator.fullSnapshot("/", depth: 2) → buildTree → onPartialUpdate → UI shows
4. FolderSizeCalculator.fullSnapshot("/", depth: 5) → buildTree → final root
5. DiskIndex.install(root)
6. User clicks folder → navigateInto → resolve from DiskIndex → optional scanFolder if children empty
7. User drops item in basket → deleteBasketContents → FileDeletionService → rescan

## Priority root folders (Macintosh HD)

/Users, /System, /Applications, /Library, /private

Defined in SystemPaths.macintoshHDPriorityPaths.

## Conventions for AI edits

- Do not reintroduce App Sandbox without explicit request
- Prefer single du calls over per-path Process spawns
- Keep DiskAnalyzerViewModel/UI unchanged unless UI task specified
- FileNode.kind drives navigation: hiddenSpace and freeSpace are not navigable
- Special virtual URLs: /.cleantree/<identifier> via SystemPaths.specialURL
- Ukrainian strings in UI are intentional (target locale)

## Build

open CleanTreeMac.xcodeproj && xcodebuild -scheme CleanTreeMac -configuration Debug build

## Links

- README: README.md
- Agent guide: AGENTS.md
